channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Federal judge rules not to immediately block DOGE access to Labor Department systems
item.description: A federal judge on Friday said he would not immediately block the Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Labor Department systems.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump is signing up local law officers to help with immigration enforcement
item.description: Republican lawmakers in about dozen states are advancing proposals that could aid President Donald Trump's deportation efforts.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Trump administration is throwing more Pentagon reporters out of their workspaces
item.description: The Trump administration is doubling down on its new policy to evict some news organizations out of their workspaces at the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democratic AGs from 19 states sue Trump admin over DOGE access to sensitive, personal data at Treasury
item.description: A federal lawsuit filed by 19 Democratic state attorneys general claims Elon Musk's DOGE violated the law by accessing American's personal data from the Treasury Department.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: West Virginia leads 22 states suing over New York Climate Change Superfund Act
item.description: West Virginia led the charge against New York’s Climate Change Superfund Act, suing to block the law.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, Fox 5

item.title: Trump orders freeze of aid to South Africa, citing country's land expropriation law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Friday formalizing his announcement earlier this week that he’ll freeze assistance to South Africa for a law aiming to address some of the wrongs of South Africa’s racist apartheid era — a law the White House says amounts to discrimi
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk's next target? Trump says DOGE will look at Department of Education, Pentagon funding
item.description: President Donald Trump said he has instructed Elon Musk to look into wasteful government spending at the Department of Education and the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk team’s access to student loan systems raises alarm over borrowers' personal information
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats are pushing back against Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency as it turns its attention to the Education Department, with lawmakers raising concerns about DOGE’s access to internal systems containing personal information on tens of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WA Democrats reportedly block move to nix 48-hour scholastic assault reporting requirement
item.description: A bill in Washington state to overhaul the Parents Bill of Rights caused controversy this week after a 48-hour sexual assault notification rule was nixed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE was tasked with stopping Treasury payments to USAID, AP sources say
item.description: Officials working with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency sought access to the U.S. Department of Treasury payment system to stop money from flowing to the U.S. Agency for International Development, according to two people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: How Trump's federal worker buyout plan could impact San Diego
item.description: As the deadline looms for workers to accept the Trump administration's sweeping separation offer, the more than two million civilian employees by the federal government across the country are weighing whether to take it.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: More of Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is picking up pace, with some confirmed by the Senate and hearings scheduled for more of his Cabinet picks. Some are already at work.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Some federal health websites restored, others still down, after data purge
item.description: While some information has been restored, scientists are still alarmed over the removal of data. It’s not clear what has changed, and some pages remain offline.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Trump doubles down on Gaza Strip idea, says no US soldiers needed
item.description: President Trump on Thursday doubled down on his proposal for the U.S. to take control of the Gaza Strip and rebuild it.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump’s Gaza Strip proposal reverberates
item.description: Aftershocks reverberated Wednesday from President Trump’s declaration the previous day that the United States should take “long-term ownership” of Gaza and that the Palestinians who live there should be moved.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: Why CBS stands at the epicenter of Trump's assault on the media
item.description: CBS News appears likely to settle a lawsuit brought by President Trump in a case that legal observers say it should win. The network is caught up in Trump's broader assault on the media and an owner intent on finalizing a sale.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Protesters rally against Trump's stop-work order for USAID
item.description: USAID and nonprofit workers gathered near the Capitol in D.C. to protest Elon Musk's efforts to shut down the aid agency with his Department of Government Efficiency, or DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Renters' rights and 13 more bills to watch at the 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: Lawmakers are busy in Carson City drawing up new rules to live by, including tougher penalties for committing crimes, protections for transgender Nevadans and a variety of laws related to housing and renters' rights.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada proposal would add selling deadly fentanyl to first-degree murder charge
item.description: A proposal at the Nevada Legislature would amend a charge of first-degree murder to include providing fentanyl to a person who dies from an overdose.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NJ lawmakers create new bill to combat impersonating first responders
item.description: WEST WINDSOR, N.J. (PIX11) - First responders can be some of the most trustworthy figures in our communities, but some will try to impersonate them to commit other crimes.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Trump unleashes a supercharged MAGA agenda and Republicans come aboard
item.description: President Donald Trump is testing how far Republicans are willing to go in supporting his supercharged “Make America Great Again” agenda, tearing down government agencies and taking actions fundamentally at odds with once-traditional party principles.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: People gather at Minnesota Capitol in nationwide protest against President Trump
item.description: Protesters marched to the Capitol from St. Paul College, carrying signs denouncing policies on immigration, LGBTQ+ rights and federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina and Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Walz sets special election for seat in suburban Ramsey County that will decide House power
item.description: A special election will fill a seat won by a Democrat in the November election that came open after a residency challenge. The result could return the Minnesota House to a tie or boost a GOP majority.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Freshman Democrat, physician Rep. Morrison warns against RFK Jr. nomination
item.description: Minnesota Third-District Democratic Rep. Kelly Morrison, who is one of a handful of physicians in Congress, joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer to express her concerns and what Kennedy’s confirmation could mean in practice.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Local congressman reacts to inspectors general dismissed by President Trump
item.description: In the flurry of Trump's executive orders covering immigration raids, federal firings, funding freezes and tariff negotiations, the dismissing of 17 inspector generals has gotten little notice, but according to San Diego Congressman Mike Levin, this is a bigger issue than most understand.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Senate was a battleground in 2023, new Republican minority leader emphasizes setting priorities
item.description: The Nevada Senate is ready to get down to business, and the end of the 2023 Legislature is still fresh in the minds of Democrats and Republicans gathered in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NJ bill looks to give veteran nonprofits a boost
item.description: UNION, N.J. (PIX11) - Almost any veteran non-profit organization would tell you that more money means more critical services they can provide to those who've served our country. New Jersey Assemblywoman Tennille McCoy is authoring a bill that would hope to give certain veteran nonprofits a hand.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: DeSantis, Florida GOP tensions spill out into open
item.description: Tensions are spilling out into the open between Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) and members of his own party in the Sunshine State, pointing to a new discordant era of GOP-led politics in a state that has trended red over the last few cycles.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC
